description: Execute a list of sub-tasks. IMPORTANT: unlike execute_command.async, this 'async' parameter controls whether sub-tasks run concurrently or sequentially — the function ALWAYS returns immediately with a task_id in both modes. async=true: sub-tasks run in parallel (no order guarantee). async=false (default): sub-tasks run one-by-one in array order. Use query_task to poll results. Example (sequential): {"items": [{"tool":"write_file","args":{"path":"a.txt","content":"hello"}}, {"tool":"read_file","args":{"path":"a.txt"}}]}
name: run_tasks

items.description: Array of sub-tasks to execute. Each item: {tool, args}. Valid tools: write_file, read_file, execute_command, call_llm, patch_file, glob, grep, search, web_fetch, process_task_status, process_wait, query_task, rename_file, delete_file, run_tasks. Note: run_tasks ALWAYS returns immediately — even async=false only means sub-tasks are sequential, not that the function blocks.

async.description: Controls sub-task scheduling, NOT whether this function blocks (it never blocks). true=parallel (concurrent), false=sequential (one by one in array order). Default: false.
